   223	
   224	fn frame(f: Frame) -> TransferFrame {
   225	    TransferFrame { frame: Some(f) }
   226	}
   227	
   228	fn error_frame(fault: &SessionFault) -> TransferFrame {
   229	    frame(Frame::Error(fault.to_wire()))
   230	}
   231	
   232	/// Short frame identifier for protocol-violation messages.
   233	fn frame_name(f: &Option<Frame>) -> &'static str {
   234	    match f {
   235	        Some(Frame::Hello(_)) => "SessionHello",
   236	        Some(Frame::Open(_)) => "SessionOpen",
   237	        Some(Frame::Accept(_)) => "SessionAccept",
   238	        Some(Frame::ManifestEntry(_)) => "ManifestEntry",
   239	        Some(Frame::ManifestComplete(_)) => "ManifestComplete",
   240	        Some(Frame::NeedBatch(_)) => "NeedBatch",
   241	        Some(Frame::NeedComplete(_)) => "NeedComplete",
   242	        Some(Frame::BlockHashes(_)) => "BlockHashList",
   243	        Some(Frame::FileBegin(_)) => "FileBegin",
   244	        Some(Frame::FileData(_)) => "FileData",
   245	        Some(Frame::TarShardHeader(_)) => "TarShardHeader",
   246	        Some(Frame::TarShardChunk(_)) => "TarShardChunk",
   247	        Some(Frame::TarShardComplete(_)) => "TarShardComplete",
   248	        Some(Frame::Block(_)) => "BlockTransfer",
   249	        Some(Frame::BlockComplete(_)) => "BlockTransferComplete",
   250	        Some(Frame::Resize(_)) => "DataPlaneResize",
   251	        Some(Frame::ResizeAck(_)) => "DataPlaneResizeAck",
   252	        Some(Frame::SourceDone(_)) => "SourceDone",
   253	        Some(Frame::Summary(_)) => "TransferSummary",
   254	        Some(Frame::Error(_)) => "SessionError",
   255	        None => "empty frame",
   256	    }
   257	}
   258	
   259	fn complement(role: TransferRole) -> TransferRole {
   260	    match role {
   261	        TransferRole::Source => TransferRole::Destination,
   262	        TransferRole::Destination => TransferRole::Source,
   263	        TransferRole::Unspecified => TransferRole::Unspecified,
   264	    }
   265	}
   266	
   267	/// Build a `SessionError` frame with the given code and message — the
   268	/// wire form an end sends to tell its peer why it is aborting. Public
   269	/// so the daemon dispatcher can emit `CANCELLED` when a `CancelJob`
   270	/// fires mid-session (the session future is aborted by the select and
   271	/// cannot send it itself — otp-4a codex F1); blit-core stays the one
   272	/// owner of the frame grammar. The build-id fields are left empty:
   273	/// they are only meaningful for `BUILD_MISMATCH`.
   274	pub fn session_error_frame(code: session_error::Code, message: impl Into<String>) -> TransferFrame {
   275	    frame(Frame::Error(SessionError {
   276	        code: code as i32,
   277	        message: message.into(),
   278	        local_build_id: String::new(),
   279	        peer_build_id: String::new(),
   280	    }))
   281	}
   282	
   283	/// Per-role capability check of the operation a `SessionOpen`
   284	/// describes. otp-3 refuses what later slices implement rather than
   285	/// silently ignoring it (fail-fast; contract §Errors).
   286	type OpenValidator = dyn Fn(&SessionOpen) -> std::result::Result<(), SessionFault> + Send + Sync;
   287	
   288	/// The local endpoint a Responder resolves a received `SessionOpen`
   289	/// to. The daemon maps the wire module name + path here; a test can
   290	/// hand a fixed root with no module semantics via
   291	/// [`DestinationTarget::Fixed`] instead.
   292	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   293	pub struct ResolvedEndpoint {
   294	    /// Absolute local root this end targets.
   295	    pub root: PathBuf,
   296	    /// Whether the resolved module forbids writes. A DESTINATION
   297	    /// responder refuses `READ_ONLY`; a SOURCE responder (otp-5,
   298	    /// daemon-send) does not care — reading a read-only module is fine.
   299	    pub read_only: bool,
   300	}
   301	
   302	/// Async callback a Responder uses to turn a received (and
   303	/// capability-validated) `SessionOpen` into its local endpoint. It
   304	/// lives caller-side — the daemon resolves modules and maps its own
   305	/// `tonic::Status` errors to [`SessionFault`], so blit-core stays free
   306	/// of module/Status types. A returned fault (unknown module,
   307	/// containment failure) becomes a `SessionError` at OPEN, never a
   308	/// silent close (contract §Phase state machine).
   309	pub type OpenResolver = dyn Fn(
   310	        &SessionOpen,
   311	    )
   312	        -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = std::result::Result<ResolvedEndpoint, SessionFault>> + Send>>
   313	    + Send
   314	    + Sync;
   315	
   316	/// Where a DESTINATION driver writes. `Fixed` is a root known up front
   317	/// (an initiator's own local root, or a test's temp dir). `Resolve`
   318	/// defers to a caller callback that maps the received `SessionOpen` to
   319	/// a local root — the daemon path, where the root depends on the wire
   320	/// module name and so can only be resolved mid-handshake (after HELLO,
   321	/// before SessionAccept). A `Resolve` target is meaningful only on a
   322	/// Responder; an Initiator always knows its own root.
   323	pub enum DestinationTarget {
   324	    Fixed(PathBuf),
   325	    Resolve(Box<OpenResolver>),
   326	}
   327	
   328	fn source_open_validator(open: &SessionOpen) -> std::result::Result<(), SessionFault> {
   329	    if open.resume.as_ref().is_some_and(|r| r.enabled) {
   330	        return Err(SessionFault::internal(
   331	            "resume is not implemented on the unified session yet (otp-7)",
   332	        ));
   333	    }
   334	    if open
   335	        .filter
   336	        .as_ref()
   337	        .is_some_and(|f| *f != FilterSpec::default())
   338	    {
   339	        return Err(SessionFault::internal(
   340	            "filters are not implemented on the unified session yet (otp-6)",
   341	        ));
   342	    }
   343	    Ok(())
   344	}
   345	
   346	fn destination_open_validator(open: &SessionOpen) -> std::result::Result<(), SessionFault> {
   347	    if open.mirror_enabled {
   348	        return Err(SessionFault::internal(
   349	            "mirror is not implemented on the unified session yet (otp-6)",
   350	        ));
   351	    }
   352	    if open.resume.as_ref().is_some_and(|r| r.enabled) {
   353	        return Err(SessionFault::internal(
   354	            "resume is not implemented on the unified session yet (otp-7)",
   355	        ));
   356	    }
   357	    Ok(())
   358	}
   359	
   360	/// Outcome of the HELLO + OPEN phases.
   361	struct Negotiated {
   362	    open: SessionOpen,
   363	    /// The responder's reply. The SOURCE initiator reads
   364	    /// `accept.data_plane` to decide dial-vs-in-stream (otp-4b).
   365	    accept: SessionAccept,
   366	    /// The write root a Responder's [`OpenResolver`] produced from the
   367	    /// received open, if one was supplied; `None` for an Initiator or a
   368	    /// fixed-root Responder (the caller supplies the root then).
   369	    resolved_root: Option<PathBuf>,
   370	    /// The bound data-plane listener + credentials a DESTINATION
   371	    /// Responder prepared before its `SessionAccept` (otp-4b). `None`
   372	    /// on an Initiator, or when the responder granted no data plane
   373	    /// (in-stream carrier). Consumed by the DESTINATION accept loop.
   374	    responder_data_plane: Option<data_plane::ResponderDataPlane>,
   375	}
   376	
   377	/// HELLO + OPEN/ACCEPT, one implementation both roles call (otp-3
   378	/// scoping requirement). Sends the refusal `SessionError` itself when
   379	/// it detects the fault locally; returned faults are `peer_notified`.
   380	async fn establish(
   381	    transport: &mut FrameTransport,
   382	    hello: &HelloConfig,
   383	    endpoint: &SessionEndpoint,
   384	    local_role: TransferRole,
   385	    validate_open: &OpenValidator,
   386	    // Consulted only on the Responder branch, after the received open
   387	    // passes `validate_open` and before SessionAccept. `None` = the
   388	    // caller supplies the root itself (Initiator, or fixed-root test).
   389	    resolve_open: Option<&OpenResolver>,
   390	) -> Result<Negotiated> {
   391	    // HELLO both ways, exact match (D-2026-07-05-2). First frame each
   392	    // direction; no ordering between the two directions.
   393	    transport
   394	        .send(frame(Frame::Hello(SessionHello {
   395	            build_id: hello.build_id.clone(),
   396	            contract_version: hello.contract_version,
   397	        })))
   398	        .await?;
   399	
   400	    let peer_hello = match expect_frame(transport).await? {
   401	        Frame::Hello(h) => h,
   402	        other => {
   403	            return Err(notify_and_wrap(
   404	                transport,
   405	                SessionFault::protocol_violation(format!(
   406	                    "expected SessionHello, got {}",
   407	                    frame_name(&Some(other))
   408	                )),
   409	            )
   410	            .await)
   411	        }
   412	    };
   413	
   414	    if peer_hello.build_id != hello.build_id
   415	        || peer_hello.contract_version != hello.contract_version
   416	    {
   417	        let fault = SessionFault {
   418	            code: session_error::Code::BuildMismatch,
   419	            message: format!(
   420	                "same-build peers required (D-2026-07-05-2): local {} (contract v{}) vs peer {} (contract v{})",
   421	                hello.build_id, hello.contract_version,
   422	                peer_hello.build_id, peer_hello.contract_version,
   423	            ),
   424	            local_build_id: hello.build_id.clone(),
   425	            peer_build_id: peer_hello.build_id.clone(),
   426	            peer_notified: false,
   427	        };
   428	        return Err(notify_and_wrap(transport, fault).await);
   429	    }
   430	
   431	    match endpoint {
   432	        SessionEndpoint::Initiator { open } => {
   433	            let open = open.as_ref().clone();
   434	            transport.send(frame(Frame::Open(open.clone()))).await?;
   435	            let accept = match expect_frame(transport).await? {
   436	                Frame::Accept(a) => a,
   437	                other => {
   438	                    return Err(notify_and_wrap(
   439	                        transport,
   440	                        SessionFault::protocol_violation(format!(
   441	                            "expected SessionAccept, got {}",
   442	                            frame_name(&Some(other))
   443	                        )),
   444	                    )
   445	                    .await)
   446	                }
   447	            };
   448	            Ok(Negotiated {
   449	                open,
   450	                accept,
   451	                resolved_root: None,
   452	                responder_data_plane: None,
   453	            })
   454	        }
   455	        SessionEndpoint::Responder => {
   456	            let open = match expect_frame(transport).await? {
   457	                Frame::Open(o) => o,
   458	                other => {
   459	                    return Err(notify_and_wrap(
   460	                        transport,
   461	                        SessionFault::protocol_violation(format!(
   462	                            "expected SessionOpen, got {}",
   463	                            frame_name(&Some(other))
   464	                        )),
   465	                    )
   466	                    .await)
   467	                }
   468	            };
   469	            // The initiator declares ITS role; this responder end must
   470	            // hold the complement.
   471	            let declared =
   472	                TransferRole::try_from(open.initiator_role).unwrap_or(TransferRole::Unspecified);
   473	            if declared != complement(local_role) {
   474	                return Err(notify_and_wrap(
   475	                    transport,
   476	                    SessionFault::protocol_violation(format!(
   477	                        "initiator declared role {} but this responder is {}",
   478	                        declared.as_str_name(),
   479	                        local_role.as_str_name()
   480	                    )),
   481	                )
   482	                .await);
   483	            }
   484	            if let Err(fault) = validate_open(&open) {
   485	                // Refusal is a SessionError instead of SessionAccept,
   486	                // never a silent close (contract §Phase state machine).
   487	                return Err(notify_and_wrap(transport, fault).await);
   488	            }
   489	            // Responder endpoint resolution (otp-4): map the wire
   490	            // module/path to a local root and enforce read-only, both
   491	            // BEFORE SessionAccept so a refusal replaces the accept
   492	            // (never follows it). The resolver is caller-supplied
   493	            // (daemon module lookup); a fixed-root responder passes
   494	            // None and resolves nothing here.
   495	            let resolved_root = match resolve_open {
   496	                Some(resolve) => match resolve(&open).await {
   497	                    Ok(resolved) => {
   498	                        // A read-only module is fatal only for a
   499	                        // DESTINATION (it would write); a SOURCE
   500	                        // responder (otp-5, daemon-send) reads happily.
   501	                        if local_role == TransferRole::Destination && resolved.read_only {
   502	                            return Err(notify_and_wrap(
   503	                                transport,
   504	                                SessionFault::read_only(
   505	                                    "destination module is read-only".to_string(),
   506	                                ),
   507	                            )
   508	                            .await);
   509	                        }
   510	                        Some(resolved.root)
   511	                    }
   512	                    Err(fault) => return Err(notify_and_wrap(transport, fault).await),
   513	                },
   514	                None => None,
   515	            };
   516	            // Data plane (otp-4b): a DESTINATION responder binds a TCP
   517	            // listener and grants it, unless the initiator requested the
   518	            // in-stream carrier or the bind fails (grant-less accept ⇒
   519	            // in-stream fallback). A SOURCE responder (otp-5,
   520	            // daemon-send) will bind on its own branch later; otp-4b's
   521	            // responder is always the DESTINATION.
   522	            let responder_data_plane =
   523	                if local_role == TransferRole::Destination && !open.in_stream_bytes {
   524	                    data_plane::prepare_responder_data_plane().await
   525	                } else {
   526	                    None
   527	                };
   528	            let accept = SessionAccept {
   529	                // The byte RECEIVER advertises capacity at session
   530	                // open (D-2026-06-20-1/-2); consumed by the dial when
   531	                // the data plane lands (otp-4b).
   532	                receiver_capacity: if local_role == TransferRole::Destination {
   533	                    Some(crate::engine::local_receiver_capacity())
   534	                } else {
   535	                    None
   536	                },
   537	                // Grant present ⇒ TCP data plane; absent ⇒ in-stream.
   538	                data_plane: responder_data_plane.as_ref().map(|dp| dp.grant()),
   539	            };
   540	            transport.send(frame(Frame::Accept(accept.clone()))).await?;
   541	            Ok(Negotiated {
   542	                open,
   543	                accept,
   544	                resolved_root,
   545	                responder_data_plane,
   546	            })
   547	        }
   548	    }
   549	}
   550	
   551	/// Receive one frame during establish; peer errors and closes become
   552	/// terminal faults.
   553	async fn expect_frame(transport: &mut FrameTransport) -> Result<Frame> {
   554	    match transport.recv().await? {
   555	        Some(TransferFrame {
   556	            frame: Some(Frame::Error(err)),
   557	        }) => Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::from_wire(err))),
   558	        Some(TransferFrame { frame: Some(f) }) => Ok(f),
   559	        Some(TransferFrame { frame: None }) => Err(eyre::Report::new(
   560	            SessionFault::protocol_violation("frame with empty oneof"),

exec
/usr/bin/zsh -lc "git show 46cc4bb:crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs | nl -ba | sed -n '560,820p'" in /home/michael/dev/Blit
 succeeded in 0ms:
   560	            SessionFault::protocol_violation("frame with empty oneof"),
   561	        )),
   562	        None => Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::internal(
   563	            "peer closed during session establish",
   564	        ))),
   565	    }
   566	}
   567	
   568	/// Send the fault to the peer (best effort), mark it notified, and
   569	/// wrap it for return.
   570	async fn notify_and_wrap(transport: &mut FrameTransport, mut fault: SessionFault) -> eyre::Report {
   571	    let _ = transport.send(error_frame(&fault)).await;
   572	    fault.peer_notified = true;
   573	    eyre::Report::new(fault)
   574	}
   575	
   576	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   577	// SOURCE driver
   578	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   579	
   580	/// Events the source's receive half forwards to its send half. The
   581	/// channel is unbounded but bounded by construction: every `Need`
   582	/// consumes a distinct sent-manifest entry (unknown or repeated paths
   583	/// fault the session), so the queue never exceeds the source's own
   584	/// manifest size — the contract's bounded-buffering rule holds.
   585	enum SourceEvent {
   586	    Need(FileHeader),
   587	    NeedComplete,
   588	    /// The destination's ack of a `DataPlaneResize{ADD}` (otp-4b-2). The
   589	    /// send half dials the epoch-N socket on `accepted`.
   590	    ResizeAck(DataPlaneResizeAck),
   591	    Summary(TransferSummary),
   592	    Fault(SessionFault),
   593	}
   594	
   595	/// Run the SOURCE role of one transfer session over `transport`.
   596	/// Returns the destination-computed `TransferSummary` (contract: the
   597	/// end that wrote the bytes is the end that attests to them).
   598	pub async fn run_source(
   599	    cfg: SourceSessionConfig,
   600	    transport: FrameTransport,
   601	    source: Arc<dyn TransferSource>,
   602	) -> Result<TransferSummary> {
   603	    let mut transport = transport;
   604	    if let SessionEndpoint::Initiator { open } = &cfg.endpoint {
   605	        // Own-config coherence: a source initiator declares SOURCE.
   606	        let declared = TransferRole::try_from(open.initiator_role);
   607	        if declared != Ok(TransferRole::Source) {
   608	            eyre::bail!("run_source initiator must declare TRANSFER_ROLE_SOURCE in SessionOpen");
   609	        }
   610	        if let Err(fault) = source_open_validator(open) {
   611	            eyre::bail!("run_source initiator config unsupported: {fault}");
   612	        }
   613	    }
   614	
   615	    let negotiated = establish(
   616	        &mut transport,
   617	        &cfg.hello,
   618	        &cfg.endpoint,
   619	        TransferRole::Source,
   620	        &source_open_validator,
   621	        // A SOURCE responder's endpoint resolution (module→root for a
   622	        // daemon-send) lands with otp-5; otp-4a's daemon is always the
   623	        // DESTINATION responder, so the source never resolves here.
   624	        None,
   625	    )
   626	    .await?;
   627	
   628	    let (mut tx, rx) = transport.split();
   629	    let sent: Arc<StdMutex<HashMap<String, FileHeader>>> = Arc::default();
   630	    // Set by the send half the moment ManifestComplete goes out. On
   631	    // an ordered transport, a NeedComplete arriving while this is
   632	    // still false is provably premature — the peer cannot have
   633	    // received what we have not sent (contract: NeedComplete only
   634	    // after ManifestComplete received + all entries diffed).
   635	    let manifest_sent = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));
   636	    let (event_tx, event_rx) = mpsc::unbounded_channel();
   637	    // AbortOnDrop: an early error return below must abort the receive
   638	    // half instead of leaking it (same rationale as design-2 / w4-1).
   639	    let _recv_guard = AbortOnDrop::new(tokio::spawn(source_recv_half(
   640	        rx,
   641	        Arc::clone(&sent),
   642	        Arc::clone(&manifest_sent),
   643	        event_tx,
   644	    )));
   645	
   646	    match source_send_half(
   647	        &cfg,
   648	        &negotiated,
   649	        &mut tx,
   650	        source,
   651	        sent,
   652	        &manifest_sent,
   653	        event_rx,
   654	    )
   655	    .await
   656	    {
   657	        Ok(summary) => Ok(summary),
   658	        Err(report) => {
   659	            let mut fault = fault_from_report(report);
   660	            if !fault.peer_notified {
   661	                let _ = tx.send(error_frame(&fault)).await;
   662	                fault.peer_notified = true;
   663	            }
   664	            Err(eyre::Report::new(fault))
   665	        }
   666	    }
   667	}
   668	
   669	/// Receive half of the source driver: drains the transport for the
   670	/// whole session so destination sends can never deadlock against a
   671	/// blocked source send, and routes the destination lane to the send
   672	/// half. Terminates on summary, error, close, or violation.
   673	async fn source_recv_half(
   674	    mut rx: Box<dyn FrameRx>,
   675	    sent: Arc<StdMutex<HashMap<String, FileHeader>>>,
   676	    manifest_sent: Arc<AtomicBool>,
   677	    events: mpsc::UnboundedSender<SourceEvent>,
   678	) {
   679	    loop {
   680	        let received = match rx.recv().await {
   681	            Ok(Some(f)) => f,
   682	            Ok(None) => {
   683	                let _ = events.send(SourceEvent::Fault(SessionFault::internal(
   684	                    "peer closed before TransferSummary",
   685	                )));
   686	                return;
   687	            }
   688	            Err(err) => {
   689	                let _ = events.send(SourceEvent::Fault(SessionFault::internal(format!(
   690	                    "transport receive failed: {err:#}"
   691	                ))));
   692	                return;
   693	            }
   694	        };
   695	        match received.frame {
   696	            Some(Frame::NeedBatch(batch)) => {
   697	                for entry in batch.entries {
   698	                    if entry.resume {
   699	                        let _ = events.send(SourceEvent::Fault(SessionFault::protocol_violation(
   700	                            format!(
   701	                                "resume-flagged need for '{}' in a session opened without resume",
   702	                                entry.relative_path
   703	                            ),
   704	                        )));
   705	                        return;
   706	                    }
   707	                    let header = sent
   708	                        .lock()
   709	                        .expect("sent-manifest lock poisoned")
   710	                        .remove(&entry.relative_path);
   711	                    match header {
   712	                        Some(h) => {
   713	                            let _ = events.send(SourceEvent::Need(h));
   714	                        }
   715	                        None => {
   716	                            let _ = events.send(SourceEvent::Fault(
   717	                                SessionFault::protocol_violation(format!(
   718	                                    "need for unknown or already-needed path '{}'",
   719	                                    entry.relative_path
   720	                                )),
   721	                            ));
   722	                            return;
   723	                        }
   724	                    }
   725	                }
   726	            }
   727	            Some(Frame::NeedComplete(_)) => {
   728	                if !manifest_sent.load(Ordering::Acquire) {
   729	                    // Fail fast at arrival time (otp-3 codex F2): the
   730	                    // event queue would otherwise let an early
   731	                    // NeedComplete be processed late and pass as
   732	                    // legitimate.
   733	                    let _ = events.send(SourceEvent::Fault(SessionFault::protocol_violation(
   734	                        "NeedComplete before the source's ManifestComplete",
   735	                    )));
   736	                    return;
   737	                }
   738	                let _ = events.send(SourceEvent::NeedComplete);
   739	            }
   740	            Some(Frame::ResizeAck(ack)) => {
   741	                // The destination's response to a shape-resize proposal
   742	                // (otp-4b-2). Forward it to the send half, which owns the
   743	                // dial and dials the epoch-N socket on `accepted`.
   744	                let _ = events.send(SourceEvent::ResizeAck(ack));
   745	            }
   746	            Some(Frame::Summary(summary)) => {
   747	                let _ = events.send(SourceEvent::Summary(summary));
   748	                return;
   749	            }
   750	            Some(Frame::Error(err)) => {
   751	                let _ = events.send(SourceEvent::Fault(SessionFault::from_wire(err)));
   752	                return;
   753	            }
   754	            other => {
   755	                let _ = events.send(SourceEvent::Fault(SessionFault::protocol_violation(
   756	                    format!("{} on the source's receive lane", frame_name(&other)),
   757	                )));
   758	                return;
   759	            }
   760	        }
   761	    }
   762	}
   763	
   764	async fn source_send_half(
   765	    cfg: &SourceSessionConfig,
   766	    negotiated: &Negotiated,
   767	    tx: &mut Box<dyn FrameTx>,
   768	    source: Arc<dyn TransferSource>,
   769	    sent: Arc<StdMutex<HashMap<String, FileHeader>>>,
   770	    manifest_sent: &AtomicBool,
   771	    mut events: mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<SourceEvent>,
   772	) -> Result<TransferSummary> {
   773	    let mut pending: Vec<FileHeader> = Vec::new();
   774	    let mut need_complete = false;
   775	
   776	    // Data plane (otp-4b): dial the granted TCP sockets up front —
   777	    // BEFORE streaming the manifest — so the destination's accept loop
   778	    // (armed the moment it sent SessionAccept) sees the connections
   779	    // promptly rather than waiting out its bounded-accept timeout while
   780	    // a long manifest streams. The sockets sit idle (keepalive covers
   781	    // that) until payloads are queued below. `None` = the in-stream
   782	    // carrier (fallback), which needs no early setup.
   783	    let mut data_plane = match &negotiated.accept.data_plane {
   784	        Some(grant) => {
   785	            let host = cfg.data_plane_host.as_deref().ok_or_else(|| {
   786	                eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::internal(
   787	                    "responder granted a TCP data plane but this initiator has no host to dial",
   788	                ))
   789	            })?;
   790	            Some(
   791	                data_plane::dial_source_data_plane(
   792	                    host,
   793	                    grant,
   794	                    negotiated.accept.receiver_capacity.as_ref(),
   795	                    Arc::clone(&source),
   796	                )

   803	    // sf-2 shape correction (otp-4b-2): running totals of the need list,

  1250	    source: &Arc<dyn TransferSource>,
  1251	    plan_options: PlanOptions,
  1252	    batch: Vec<FileHeader>,
  1253	    read_buf: &mut [u8],
  1254	) -> Result<()> {
  1255	    let payloads = diff_planner::plan_push_payloads(batch, source.root(), plan_options)?;
  1256	    for payload in payloads {
  1257	        match source.prepare_payload(payload).await? {
  1258	            PreparedPayload::File(header) => {
  1259	                tx.send(frame(Frame::FileBegin(header.clone()))).await?;
  1260	                if header.size == 0 {
  1261	                    continue; // record complete at 0 cumulative bytes
  1262	                }
  1263	                let mut reader = source.open_file(&header).await?;
  1264	                let mut remaining = header.size;
  1265	                while remaining > 0 {
  1266	                    let want = read_buf.len().min(remaining as usize);
  1267	                    let got = reader.read(&mut read_buf[..want]).await?;
  1268	                    if got == 0 {
  1269	                        // Shorter on disk than the manifest promised —
  1270	                        // the record can no longer complete at
  1271	                        // header.size; abort rather than pad.
  1272	                        eyre::bail!(
  1273	                            "'{}' hit EOF with {} bytes still promised",
  1274	                            header.relative_path,
  1275	                            remaining
  1276	                        );
  1277	                    }
  1278	                    tx.send(frame(Frame::FileData(FileData {
  1279	                        content: read_buf[..got].to_vec(),
  1280	                    })))
  1281	                    .await?;
  1282	                    remaining -= got as u64;
  1283	                }
  1284	            }
  1285	            PreparedPayload::TarShard { headers, data } => {
  1286	                tx.send(frame(Frame::TarShardHeader(TarShardHeader {
  1287	                    files: headers,
  1288	                    archive_size: data.len() as u64,
  1289	                })))

  1293	                        crate::generated::TarShardChunk {
  1294	                            content: chunk.to_vec(),
  1295	                        },
  1296	                    )))
  1297	                    .await?;
  1298	                }
  1299	                tx.send(frame(Frame::TarShardComplete(TarShardComplete {})))
  1300	                    .await?;
  1301	            }

  1303	                // The outbound planner never emits these (resume is
  1304	                // receive-originated and lands at otp-7).
  1305	                eyre::bail!("resume payload planned in a non-resume session");
  1306	            }
  1307	        }
  1308	    }
  1309	    Ok(())

  1312	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1313	// DESTINATION driver
  1314	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1315	
  1316	/// What the destination end can report after a completed session.
  1317	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
  1318	pub struct DestinationOutcome {
  1319	    /// The summary this end computed and sent (contract: DESTINATION
  1320	    /// is the scorer).
  1321	    pub summary: TransferSummary,
  1322	    /// Paths this end put on the need list, in emission order. The
  1323	    /// role suite pins these identical across role assignments — the
  1324	    /// executable form of the owner's invariance requirement.
  1325	    pub needed_paths: Vec<String>,
  1326	    /// The settled data-plane stream count this end observed (epoch-0 +
  1327	    /// accepted resizes), or `None` for the in-stream carrier. The sf-2
  1328	    /// pin (otp-4b-2) reads it to assert shape correction grew the
  1329	    /// stream set past the zero-knowledge single-stream grant.
  1330	    pub data_plane_streams: Option<usize>,
  1331	}
  1332	
  1333	/// Run the DESTINATION role of one transfer session over `transport`,
  1334	/// writing under the root named by `target`. Diffs the streamed
  1335	/// manifest against its own filesystem (the destination is the one
  1336	/// diff owner — plan §Design 3), returns the summary it computed and
  1337	/// sent.
  1338	///
  1339	/// `target` is [`DestinationTarget::Fixed`] when the root is known up
  1340	/// front (an Initiator's own local root, or a test), or
  1341	/// [`DestinationTarget::Resolve`] when the root must be resolved from
  1342	/// the received `SessionOpen` mid-handshake (the daemon Responder,
  1343	/// where the wire module name selects the root).
  1344	pub async fn run_destination(
  1345	    cfg: DestinationSessionConfig,
  1346	    transport: FrameTransport,
  1347	    target: DestinationTarget,
  1348	) -> Result<DestinationOutcome> {
  1349	    let mut transport = transport;
  1350	    let endpoint = match cfg.endpoint {
  1351	        SessionEndpoint::Initiator { mut open } => {
  1352	            let declared = TransferRole::try_from(open.initiator_role);
  1353	            if declared != Ok(TransferRole::Destination) {
  1354	                eyre::bail!(
  1355	                    "run_destination initiator must declare TRANSFER_ROLE_DESTINATION in SessionOpen"
  1356	                );
  1357	            }
  1358	            if let Err(fault) = destination_open_validator(&open) {
  1359	                eyre::bail!("run_destination initiator config unsupported: {fault}");
  1360	            }
  1361	            // Dial contract: the byte receiver advertises capacity in
  1362	            // its open when it is the initiator (contract §Invariants 5).
  1363	            if open.receiver_capacity.is_none() {
  1364	                open.receiver_capacity = Some(crate::engine::local_receiver_capacity());
  1365	            }
  1366	            SessionEndpoint::Initiator { open }
  1367	        }
  1368	        SessionEndpoint::Responder => SessionEndpoint::Responder,
  1369	    };
  1370	
  1371	    let resolve_open: Option<&OpenResolver> = match &target {
  1372	        DestinationTarget::Resolve(resolver) => Some(resolver.as_ref()),
  1373	        DestinationTarget::Fixed(_) => None,
  1374	    };
  1375	
  1376	    let negotiated = establish(
  1377	        &mut transport,
  1378	        &cfg.hello,
  1379	        &endpoint,
  1380	        TransferRole::Destination,
  1381	        &destination_open_validator,
  1382	        resolve_open,
  1383	    )
  1384	    .await?;
  1385	
  1386	    // The resolver's root (Responder + Resolve) wins; otherwise the
  1387	    // caller-supplied Fixed root.
  1388	    let dst_root = match negotiated.resolved_root.clone() {
  1389	        Some(root) => root,
  1390	        None => match &target {
  1391	            DestinationTarget::Fixed(root) => root.clone(),
  1392	            // Unreachable: a Resolve target always yields a root on the
  1393	            // Responder branch, and establish only skips resolution on
  1394	            // the Initiator branch (which pairs with a Fixed root).
  1395	            DestinationTarget::Resolve(_) => {
  1396	                return Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::internal(
  1397	                    "resolver target produced no destination root",
  1398	                )));
  1399	            }
  1400	        },
  1401	    };
  1402	
  1403	    match destination_session(&mut transport, negotiated, &dst_root).await {
  1404	        Ok(outcome) => Ok(outcome),
  1405	        Err(report) => {
  1406	            let mut fault = fault_from_report(report);
  1407	            if !fault.peer_notified {
  1408	                let _ = transport.send(error_frame(&fault)).await;
  1409	                fault.peer_notified = true;
  1410	            }
  1411	            Err(eyre::Report::new(fault))
  1412	        }
  1413	    }
  1414	}
  1415	
  1416	fn violation(message: String) -> eyre::Report {
  1417	    eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::protocol_violation(message))
  1418	}
  1419	
  1420	async fn destination_session(
  1421	    transport: &mut FrameTransport,
  1422	    negotiated: Negotiated,
  1423	    dst_root: &Path,
  1424	) -> Result<DestinationOutcome> {
  1425	    let compare_mode = ComparisonMode::try_from(negotiated.open.compare_mode)
  1426	        .unwrap_or(ComparisonMode::Unspecified);
  1427	    let compare_opts = CompareOptions {
  1428	        mode: compare_mode.into(),
  1429	        ignore_existing: negotiated.open.ignore_existing,
  1430	        include_deletions: false, // mirror lands at otp-6
  1431	    };
  1432	    // src_root is only consumed by local File payloads, which never
  1433	    // occur on a session destination (payload bytes arrive as records
  1434	    // and go through the stream/tar write paths). `Arc` so the data-plane
  1435	    // receive task (otp-4b) can share the one sink across sockets.
  1436	    let sink = Arc::new(FsTransferSink::new(
  1437	        PathBuf::new(),
  1438	        dst_root.to_path_buf(),
  1439	        FsSinkConfig {
  1440	            preserve_times: true,
  1441	            dry_run: false,
  1442	            checksum: None,
  1443	            resume: false,
  1444	            compare_mode,
  1445	        },
  1446	    ));
  1447	    // Same canonical-containment chokepoint the sink write paths use
  1448	    // (R46-F3), applied to diff stats so a hostile manifest path can't
  1449	    // make the destination stat outside its root.
  1450	    let canonical_dst_root = crate::path_safety::canonical_dest_root(dst_root).ok();
  1451	
  1452	    // Two sets, deliberately separate (codex otp-4b-1 fix-review F1):
  1453	    // `granted` is the ever-granted DEDUP set — control-loop-local,
  1454	    // insert-only, never removed, so a concurrent data-plane claim can
  1455	    // never re-open a grant (a duplicate manifest path is granted at
  1456	    // most once regardless of delivery timing). `outstanding` is the
  1457	    // not-yet-delivered COMPLETION set — inserted for each freshly
  1458	    // granted path before its NeedBatch, claimed by both carriers (the
  1459	    // in-stream arms inline, the data-plane NeedListSink as payloads
  1460	    // land), and empty at SourceDone. A count proxy was insufficient
  1461	    // (F1); merging the two into one set raced the data-plane claim
  1462	    // against the diff (fix-review F1).
  1463	    let mut granted: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();
  1464	    let outstanding: data_plane::OutstandingNeeds = Arc::new(StdMutex::new(HashSet::new()));
  1465	
  1466	    // Data plane (otp-4b): when the responder granted a TCP data plane,
  1467	    // payload bytes arrive on sockets (not the control lane). Arm the
  1468	    // accept+receive task NOW — concurrent with the diff loop below, and
  1469	    // before the source dials — so the connections are accepted promptly.
  1470	    // The NeedListSink gives the socket receive the same need-list
  1471	    // strictness the in-stream control loop applies inline. AbortOnDrop
  1472	    // bounds it to this future: a control-lane fault that returns from
  1473	    // this fn aborts the receive task instead of leaking it.
  1474	    // `resize_live` tracks the stream count this end has granted (epoch-0
  1475	    // plus each accepted resize ADD); `resize_ceiling` is the receiver's
  1476	    // advertised max_streams, the cumulative bound a resize may not cross.
  1477	    let (mut data_plane_recv, mut resize_live, resize_ceiling) =
  1478	        match negotiated.responder_data_plane {
  1479	            Some(rdp) => {
  1480	                let initial = rdp.initial_streams() as usize;
  1481	                let recv_sink: Arc<dyn TransferSink> = Arc::new(data_plane::NeedListSink::new(
  1482	                    Arc::clone(&sink) as Arc<dyn TransferSink>,
  1483	                    Arc::clone(&outstanding),
  1484	                ));
  1485	                let run = rdp.spawn(recv_sink);
  1486	                let ceiling = run.ceiling;
  1487	                (Some(run), initial, ceiling)
  1488	            }
  1489	            None => (None, 0usize, 0usize),
  1490	        };
  1491	
  1492	    let mut pending: Vec<FileHeader> = Vec::new();
  1493	    let mut needed_paths: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
  1494	    let mut manifest_complete = false;
  1495	    let mut files_written: u64 = 0;
  1496	    let mut bytes_written: u64 = 0;
  1497	
  1498	    loop {
  1499	        let received = match transport.recv().await? {
  1500	            Some(f) => f,
  1501	            None => {
  1502	                return Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::internal(
  1503	                    "peer closed mid-session",
  1504	                )))
  1505	            }
  1506	        };
  1507	        match received.frame {
  1508	            Some(Frame::ManifestEntry(header)) => {
  1509	                if manifest_complete {
  1510	                    return Err(violation(format!(
  1511	                        "manifest entry '{}' after ManifestComplete",
  1512	                        header.relative_path
  1513	                    )));
  1514	                }
  1515	                pending.push(header);
  1516	                if pending.len() >= DEST_DIFF_CHUNK {
  1517	                    let chunk = std::mem::take(&mut pending);
  1518	                    diff_chunk_and_send_needs(
  1519	                        transport,
  1520	                        chunk,
  1521	                        dst_root,
  1522	                        canonical_dst_root.as_deref(),
  1523	                        &compare_opts,
  1524	                        &mut granted,
  1525	                        &outstanding,
  1526	                        &mut needed_paths,
  1527	                    )
  1528	                    .await?;
  1529	                }
  1530	            }
  1531	            Some(Frame::ManifestComplete(_complete)) => {
  1532	                if manifest_complete {
  1533	                    return Err(violation("duplicate ManifestComplete".into()));
  1534	                }
  1535	                // (scan_complete gates mirror purges from otp-6 on;
  1536	                // nothing consumes it in otp-3.)
  1537	                let chunk = std::mem::take(&mut pending);
  1538	                diff_chunk_and_send_needs(
  1539	                    transport,
  1540	                    chunk,
  1541	                    dst_root,
  1542	                    canonical_dst_root.as_deref(),
  1543	                    &compare_opts,
  1544	                    &mut granted,
  1545	                    &outstanding,
  1546	                    &mut needed_paths,
  1547	                )
  1548	                .await?;
  1549	                // NeedComplete only after ManifestComplete received
  1550	                // AND every entry diffed — both true here.
  1551	                transport
  1552	                    .send(frame(Frame::NeedComplete(NeedComplete {})))
  1553	                    .await?;
  1554	                manifest_complete = true;
  1555	            }
  1556	            Some(Frame::FileBegin(header)) => {
  1557	                // Payload records ride the control lane only under the
  1558	                // in-stream carrier; with a TCP data plane active they
  1559	                // flow over the sockets, so one here is a violation.
  1560	                if data_plane_recv.is_some() {
  1561	                    return Err(violation(format!(
  1562	                        "file record '{}' on the control lane while a TCP data plane is active",
  1563	                        header.relative_path
  1564	                    )));
  1565	                }
  1566	                if !manifest_complete {
  1567	                    return Err(violation(format!(
  1568	                        "payload record for '{}' before ManifestComplete",
  1569	                        header.relative_path
  1570	                    )));
  1571	                }
  1572	                if !outstanding
  1573	                    .lock()
  1574	                    .expect("outstanding-needs lock poisoned")
  1575	                    .remove(&header.relative_path)
  1576	                {
  1577	                    return Err(violation(format!(
  1578	                        "payload for '{}' which is not on the need list",
  1579	                        header.relative_path
  1580	                    )));
  1581	                }
  1582	                let outcome = receive_file_record(transport, &sink, &header).await?;
  1583	                files_written += outcome.files_written as u64;
  1584	                bytes_written += outcome.bytes_written;
  1585	            }
  1586	            Some(Frame::TarShardHeader(shard)) => {
  1587	                if data_plane_recv.is_some() {
  1588	                    return Err(violation(
  1589	                        "tar shard record on the control lane while a TCP data plane is active"
  1590	                            .into(),
  1591	                    ));
  1592	                }
  1593	                if !manifest_complete {
  1594	                    return Err(violation("tar shard record before ManifestComplete".into()));
  1595	                }
  1596	                {
  1597	                    let mut out = outstanding.lock().expect("outstanding-needs lock poisoned");
  1598	                    for h in &shard.files {
  1599	                        if !out.remove(&h.relative_path) {
  1600	                            return Err(violation(format!(
  1601	                                "tar shard entry '{}' which is not on the need list",
  1602	                                h.relative_path
  1603	                            )));
  1604	                        }
  1605	                    }
  1606	                }
  1607	                let outcome = receive_tar_record(transport, &sink, shard).await?;
  1608	                files_written += outcome.files_written as u64;
  1609	                bytes_written += outcome.bytes_written;
  1610	            }
  1611	            Some(Frame::Resize(resize)) => {
  1612	                // sf-2 shape correction (otp-4b-2): the SOURCE proposes
  1613	                // one ADD; arm the credential, grant it (bump `resize_live`),
  1614	                // and ack so the SOURCE dials the epoch-N socket. Only ADD
  1615	                // occurs on the session (REMOVE is a tuner concern, future
  1616	                // work); anything else fails fast.
  1617	                let run = data_plane_recv.as_ref().ok_or_else(|| {
  1618	                    violation("DataPlaneResize on a session with no data plane".into())
  1619	                })?;
  1620	                let op = DataPlaneResizeOp::try_from(resize.op)
  1621	                    .unwrap_or(DataPlaneResizeOp::Unspecified);
  1622	                if op != DataPlaneResizeOp::Add {
  1623	                    return Err(violation(format!(
  1624	                        "unsupported data-plane resize op {}",
  1625	                        op.as_str_name()
  1626	                    )));
  1627	                }
  1628	                if resize.sub_token.len() != crate::remote::transfer::SUB_TOKEN_LEN {
  1629	                    return Err(violation(
  1630	                        "DataPlaneResize sub_token must be 16 bytes".into(),
  1631	                    ));
  1632	                }
  1633	                // Cumulative ceiling bound (defense in depth — the
  1634	                // source's dial already clamps to the same profile).
  1635	                let accepted = resize_live < resize_ceiling && run.arm(resize.sub_token.clone());
  1636	                if accepted {
  1637	                    resize_live += 1;
  1638	                }
  1639	                let effective = if accepted {
  1640	                    resize.target_stream_count
  1641	                } else {
  1642	                    resize_live as u32
  1643	                };
  1644	                transport
  1645	                    .send(frame(Frame::ResizeAck(DataPlaneResizeAck {
  1646	                        epoch: resize.epoch,
  1647	                        effective_stream_count: effective,
  1648	                        accepted,
  1649	                    })))
  1650	                    .await?;
  1651	            }
  1652	            Some(Frame::SourceDone(_)) => {
  1653	                if !manifest_complete {
  1654	                    return Err(violation("SourceDone before ManifestComplete".into()));
  1655	                }
  1656	                // Completion, both carriers: the shared `outstanding`
  1657	                // set must be empty (every granted need claimed exactly
  1658	                // once). In-stream claims inline above; the data-plane
  1659	                // NeedListSink claims as payloads land, so joining the
  1660	                // receive task first drains the last of them (and
  1661	                // surfaces any receive error / stall). Set membership —
  1662	                // not a file count — is the contract (codex F1: a count
  1663	                // proxy let a peer substitute or duplicate paths).
  1664	                // `finish()` drops the arm sender (no more resizes), joins
  1665	                // the accept loop, and reports the settled stream count.
  1666	                let (in_stream_carrier_used, data_plane_streams) = match data_plane_recv.take() {
  1667	                    Some(run) => {
  1668	                        let totals = run.finish().await?;
  1669	                        files_written = totals.outcome.files_written as u64;
  1670	                        bytes_written = totals.outcome.bytes_written;
  1671	                        (false, Some(totals.streams))
  1672	                    }
  1673	                    None => (true, None),
  1674	                };
  1675	                let unfulfilled = outstanding
  1676	                    .lock()
  1677	                    .expect("outstanding-needs lock poisoned")
  1678	                    .len();
  1679	                if unfulfilled != 0 {
  1680	                    return Err(violation(format!(
  1681	                        "SourceDone with {unfulfilled} needed file(s) never delivered"
  1682	                    )));
  1683	                }
  1684	                let summary = TransferSummary {
  1685	                    files_transferred: files_written,
  1686	                    bytes_transferred: bytes_written,
  1687	                    entries_deleted: 0, // mirror lands at otp-6
  1688	                    in_stream_carrier_used,
  1689	                    files_resumed: 0, // resume lands at otp-7
  1690	                };
  1691	                transport.send(frame(Frame::Summary(summary))).await?;
  1692	                return Ok(DestinationOutcome {
  1693	                    summary,
  1694	                    needed_paths,
  1695	                    data_plane_streams,
  1696	                });
  1697	            }
  1698	            Some(Frame::Error(err)) => {
  1699	                return Err(eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::from_wire(err)));
  1700	            }
  1701	            other => {
  1702	                // Everything else is off-lane or off-phase here:
  1703	                // destination-lane frames echoed back (a ResizeAck the
  1704	                // destination would never receive), resume frames in a
  1705	                // non-resume session (otp-7), stray handshake frames,
  1706	                // bare FileData/TarShardChunk outside a record. Fail
  1707	                // fast, no tolerant parsing.
  1708	                return Err(violation(format!(
  1709	                    "{} not valid on the destination's receive lane in this phase",
  1710	                    frame_name(&other)
  1711	                )));
  1712	            }
  1713	        }
  1714	    }
  1715	}
  1716	
  1717	/// Stat-and-compare one chunk of manifest entries on the blocking
  1718	/// pool (2+ syscalls per entry — same rationale as the daemon's
  1719	/// w4-4 chunked checks), then stream the resulting need batch.
  1720	async fn diff_chunk_and_send_needs(
  1721	    transport: &mut FrameTransport,
  1722	    chunk: Vec<FileHeader>,
  1723	    dst_root: &Path,
  1724	    canonical_dst_root: Option<&Path>,
  1725	    compare_opts: &CompareOptions,
  1726	    // Ever-granted DEDUP set (control-loop-local, insert-only): a path
  1727	    // the source manifests twice is granted at most once, and because it
  1728	    // is never removed, a concurrent data-plane claim can't re-open the
  1729	    // grant (fix-review F1).
  1730	    granted: &mut HashSet<String>,
  1731	    // Not-yet-delivered COMPLETION set (shared with the receive).
  1732	    outstanding: &data_plane::OutstandingNeeds,
  1733	    needed_paths: &mut Vec<String>,
  1734	) -> Result<()> {
  1735	    if chunk.is_empty() {
  1736	        return Ok(());
  1737	    }
  1738	    let dst_root = dst_root.to_path_buf();
  1739	    let canonical = canonical_dst_root.map(Path::to_path_buf);
  1740	    let opts = compare_opts.clone();
  1741	    let needed: Vec<String> = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || -> Result<Vec<String>> {
  1742	        let mut needed = Vec::new();
  1743	        for header in &chunk {
  1744	            if destination_needs(header, &dst_root, canonical.as_deref(), &opts)? {
  1745	                needed.push(header.relative_path.clone());
  1746	            }
  1747	        }
  1748	        Ok(needed)
  1749	    })
  1750	    .await
  1751	    .map_err(|err| eyre::eyre!("destination diff task panicked: {err}"))??;
  1752	
  1753	    // Dedup on the ever-granted set (no lock — control-loop-local), then
  1754	    // insert the freshly granted paths into the shared `outstanding`
  1755	    // completion set BEFORE the NeedBatch goes out. The source can only
  1756	    // send a payload after receiving its need, so insert-before-send
  1757	    // orders the data-plane receive's `claim` strictly after this insert.
  1758	    let fresh: Vec<String> = needed
  1759	        .into_iter()
  1760	        .filter(|path| granted.insert(path.clone()))
  1761	        .collect();
  1762	    let entries: Vec<NeedEntry> = {
  1763	        let mut out = outstanding.lock().expect("outstanding-needs lock poisoned");
  1764	        fresh
  1765	            .into_iter()
  1766	            .map(|relative_path| {
  1767	                needed_paths.push(relative_path.clone());
  1768	                out.insert(relative_path.clone());
  1769	                NeedEntry {
  1770	                    relative_path,
  1771	                    resume: false, // resume lands at otp-7
  1772	                }
  1773	            })
  1774	            .collect()
  1775	    };
  1776	    if entries.is_empty() {
  1777	        return Ok(());
  1778	    }
  1779	    transport
  1780	        .send(frame(Frame::NeedBatch(NeedBatch { entries })))
  1781	        .await?;
  1782	    Ok(())
  1783	}
  1784	
  1785	/// Does the destination need this manifest entry? Stats its own file
  1786	/// and delegates the verdict to `manifest::header_transfer_status` —
  1787	/// the same mode-aware owner `compare_manifests` uses, fed from a
  1788	/// live stat instead of a materialized target manifest.
  1789	fn destination_needs(
  1790	    header: &FileHeader,
  1791	    dst_root: &Path,
  1792	    canonical_dst_root: Option<&Path>,
  1793	    opts: &CompareOptions,
  1794	) -> Result<bool> {
  1795	    let dst = match canonical_dst_root {
  1796	        Some(canonical) => {
  1797	            crate::path_safety::safe_join_contained(canonical, dst_root, &header.relative_path)
  1798	        }
  1799	        None => crate::path_safety::safe_join(dst_root, &header.relative_path),
  1800	    }
  1801	    .map_err(|err| {
  1802	        SessionFault::protocol_violation(format!(
  1803	            "manifest path '{}' escapes the destination root: {err:#}",
  1804	            header.relative_path
  1805	        ))
  1806	    })?;
  1807	
  1808	    let target = match std::fs::metadata(&dst) {
  1809	        Ok(meta) if meta.is_file() => {
  1810	            let mtime = match meta.modified() {
  1811	                Ok(t) => match t.duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH) {
  1812	                    Ok(d) => d.as_secs() as i64,
  1813	                    Err(e) => -(e.duration().as_secs() as i64),
  1814	                },
  1815	                Err(_) => 0,
  1816	            };
  1817	            Some((meta.len(), mtime))
  1818	        }
  1819	        // Absent — or present as a directory/other, which a file
  1820	        // write must replace: both diff as "target does not have it"
  1821	        // (matches the push daemon's file_requires_upload).
  1822	        _ => None,
  1823	    };
  1824	    let status = header_transfer_status(
  1825	        header,
  1826	        // Destination-side checksums are never precomputed; Checksum
  1827	        // mode therefore transfers (the conservative arm of
  1828	        // compare_file), matching what push does today.
  1829	        target.map(|(size, mtime)| (size, mtime, &[] as &[u8])),
  1830	        opts,
  1831	    );
  1832	    Ok(matches!(status, FileStatus::New | FileStatus::Modified))
  1833	}
  1834	
  1835	/// Receive one strictly-serialized file record (`file_begin` already

codex
SourceEvent production is bounded by sent manifest entries plus one completion/summary/fault path; bad wire frames are converted to `Fault` before they reach the lenient helper. That makes the skip narrow: it only discards already-forwarded control-lane events while selecting the terminal cause after a data-plane failure.

46cc4bb:.review/findings/otp-4b-data-plane.md:35:`SessionError{CANCELLED}` on the control lane. But the SOURCE only
46cc4bb:.review/findings/otp-4b-data-plane.md:39:only the data-plane pipeline. So a mid-transfer cancel dropped the
46cc4bb:.review/findings/otp-4b-data-plane.md:55:  terminate as CANCELLED instead of hanging.
